sub_pixel_system.notes: No sub-pixel system data documented in available sources

special_mechanics.name: Item interaction system
special_mechanics.description: Character uses various items to interact with environment and solve puzzles. Items include weapons (boomerangs, spears) and utility items (water gun for puzzle-solving, compass for map navigation).

special_mechanics.name: Environmental interaction
special_mechanics.description: Player can climb trees, enter doors, and navigate complex scrolling map with non-linear level design.

special_mechanics.name: Life gauge system
special_mechanics.description: Character has expandable health bar allowing multiple hits. Life restored by collecting fruits and hearts scattered throughout game. Heart containers expand max health by collecting two fragments.

tier_notes: Tier 3 - Empirical/Qualitative data only. Sources document gameplay mechanics and design philosophy but contain no quantified physics parameters (speeds, acceleration, gravity values, sub-pixel precision). Game mechanics emphasis is on exploration, items, and environmental interaction rather than precise movement physics.
sources_used: tcrf - Takahashi Meijin no Boukenjima IV page
